Title: The superior allele LEA12OR in wild rice enhances salt tolerance and yield

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative cleavage step of carotenoid-derived ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: salt stress-responsive; expression reduced in lea12-1 and LEA12^93-11 overexpression plants and increased in LEA12^OR overexpression plants under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a committed precursor for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ABA accumulation under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis under salt stress; prior reported pathway evidence cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED3 is a critical enzyme in the ABA biosynthesis pathway cited by the study. 2. Its expression is reduced in lea12-1 and LEA12^93-11 overexpression plants but increased in LEA12^OR overexpression plants under salt stress. 3. It acts downstream in the LEA12OR-OsSAPK10-OsbZIP86-OsNCED3 module promoting ABA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes osmotic adjustment and salt tolerance, including stomatal regulation and stress-responsive gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not directly mutate OsNCED3 in this study; role supported partly by previous work and downstream expression analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for enhancing ABA-mediated salt tolerance, but not the main breeding gene in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 93-11; Nipponbare; Zhonghua11; NIL-LEA12OR; lea12-1 background lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: protein stability regulator; stress-response LEA protein
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LEA_3 group late embryogenesis abundant protein
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: salt-induced; highly expressed in leaf; similar expression to LEA12^93-11 under normal and salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: salt-induced; highly expressed in leaf and especially epidermal cells; similar pattern to OsSAPK10
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl salt stress; drought; saline field
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: maintains OsSAPK10 protein stability, thereby promoting downstream OsbZIP86 phosphorylation and OsNCED3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein stabilization through direct interaction; early positive regulation of ABA biosynthesis module
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis via osmotic-stress response signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: elite superior allele and early regulator of the LEA12OR-OsSAPK10-OsbZIP86-OsNCED3 module controlling salt-induced ABA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases ABA accumulation, improves osmotic adjustment, enhances salt tolerance and increases yield under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; LD analysis; NIL validation; CRISPR knockout; tilling mutant; complementation; overexpression of allelic haplotypes; ABA quantification; Co-IP; pull-down; BiFC; LCI; MST; cell-free degradation assay; field trials
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LEA12OR from Oryza rufipogon is the causal elite allele underlying major salt-tolerance QTLs on chromosome 5. 2. LEA12OR, but not LEA12^93-11, confers strong salt tolerance by improving osmotic adjustment and increasing ABA accumulation under salt stress. 3. LEA12OR directly interacts with and stabilizes OsSAPK10, promoting the OsSAPK10-OsbZIP86-OsNCED3 ABA-biosynthetic module. 4. LEA12OR improves yield per plant in saline fields.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported for plant height, grain length, grain width, or 1000-grain weight in saline field; beneficial yield effects under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GBS for QTL mapping with 32,249 SNPs; 3K rice population haplotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates osmotic adjustment, stomatal regulation, and salt stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The functional superiority is associated with a coding SNP causing L-to-V substitution at residue 307; promoter variation did not explain the phenotype. Stronger binding to OsSAPK10 distinguishes LEA12OR from LEA12^93-11.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value elite allele for molecular breeding and genome editing to improve salt tolerance and yield in rice; suitable for deployment in saline areas.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf; salt-induced in shoot
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in leaf; salt-induced in shoot
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl salt stress; MG132 proteasome inhibitor; drought referenced from prior work
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: phosphorylates OsbZIP86, which regulates OsNCED3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein kinase signaling; positive regulation of ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis and accumulation under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key kinase in the LEA12OR-OsSAPK10-OsbZIP86-OsNCED3 module required for ABA accumulation and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates ABA accumulation and salt tolerance; loss reduces ABA content and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid screening; CRISPR knockout; double mutant analysis; Co-IP; pull-down; BiFC; LCI; MST; protein degradation assays; in vitro phosphorylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSAPK10 was identified as a direct interacting partner of LEA12. 2. OsSAPK10 is genetically required for full salt tolerance and ABA accumulation. 3. LEA12OR maintains OsSAPK10 stability under salt stress, reducing proteasome-dependent degradation. 4. OsSAPK10 phosphorylates OsbZIP86 in the ABA biosynthetic regulatory cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout reduces salt tolerance and osmotic adjustment traits; no other tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA supports osmotic adjustment and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes stabilization of OsSAPK10 protein, not transcriptional upregulation, as the major LEA12OR effect.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway partner for stacking with LEA12OR to enhance ABA-mediated salt tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salt stress; in vitro phosphorylation context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: OsSAPK10 phosphorylation; LEA12OR-mediated stabilization of OsSAPK10
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates/regulates OsNCED3 expression after phosphorylation by OsSAPK10
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: downstream transcriptional regulator linking OsSAPK10 activity to OsNCED3 expression in the functional module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ABA biosynthetic gene expression downstream of OsSAPK10
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro phosphorylation assay in this study; prior pathway evidence cited
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP86 is the downstream transcription factor phosphorylated by OsSAPK10. 2. LEA12OR enhances OsSAPK10-mediated phosphorylation of OsbZIP86 by stabilizing OsSAPK10. 3. OsbZIP86 links kinase signaling to OsNCED3 expression in ABA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA supports osmotic adjustment and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly genetically manipulated in this paper; included because it is part of the experimentally supported core functional module in Results.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream regulatory target for engineering ABA-mediated salt tolerance, but not the main breeding allele here.
